name: find-replace
description: Modern find-and-replace using sd (simpler than sed) and batch replacement patterns. Triggers on: sd, find replace, batch replace, sed replacement, string replacement, rename.
capabilities: Find and replace text in single files
Batch replace across multiple files using glob patterns
Word boundary matching for safe replacements
Safe workflow patterns with version control
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with examples for the sd CLI tool. No executable code, file access, network access, or command execution capabilities. All static findings are false positives triggered by bash examples in documentation context.
valueStatement: Perform find-and-replace operations across multiple files using the sd CLI tool. The tool simplifies text replacement with regex support and safer defaults than traditional sed.
userTitle: Find and Replace with sd CLI
limitations: Requires sd CLI tool to be installed separately
Only provides documentation and examples - does not execute commands itself
Cannot perform replacements without the sd binary

bestPractices: Preview changes with ripgrep before applying bulk replacements
Use word boundaries '\b' to avoid partial matches
Commit to git before running batch replacements for easy rollback
antiPatterns: Using patterns without escaping special regex characters
Applying changes directly to production without version control

faq.question: What platforms support the sd CLI tool?
faq.answer: sd works on macOS, Linux, and Windows via WSL. Install with 'brew install sd' on macOS or 'cargo install sd' cross-platform.

faq.question: How is sd different from sed?
faq.answer: sd is global by default, uses simpler syntax without delimiters, and has better regex support without escaping slashes.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides documentation and patterns. Users must have sd installed and execute commands through their terminal.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using sd?
faq.answer: Yes. sd runs locally on your machine. No data is sent to external servers. Always preview changes before applying.

faq.question: Can sd damage my files?
faq.answer: Yes, improper patterns can cause data loss. Always preview with ripgrep first and use version control before bulk changes.

faq.question: What if I make a mistake?
faq.answer: Use git to revert changes: 'git checkout .' to undo all uncommitted changes, or 'git log' to find and restore specific versions.
